item.title: Texas Governor Seeks Consumer Protection From Data Center Costs
item.description: Texas Governor Greg Abbott called on state regulators to better protect ratepayers from shouldering the costs of the data center buildout, the latest sign of growing political scrutiny over the AI-driven surge in electricity demand.

item.title: Why Sustainable Finance Hasn’t Moved the Needle on Climate Change
item.description: A new study by Lisa Sachs, director of the Columbia Center on Sustainable Investment, lays out the problem.

item.title: NYC, Northeast Face Sweltering Heat as World Cup Matches Begin
item.description: Hot weather will settle over New York and the US Northeast through the end of the week, bringing temperatures in the 90s to several cities hosting FIFA World Cup matches this weekend.

item.title: Dutch Grid Calls for Backup Power Supply as Shortages Loom
item.description: The Dutch grid operator called for additional measures to safeguard electricity supply as surging demand and the transition from fossils fuels threaten power shortages by the turn of the decade.

item.title: El Niño Is Here and That’s Bad News for the Global Economy
item.description: The climate phenomenon arrives at a perilous time, with energy markets already strained from disruptions caused by the Iran war.

item.title: Why the World Is Bracing for a Rare ‘Super’ El Niño
item.description: ​​A cyclical weather phenomenon that can drive flooding in one continent and drought in another has returned.

item.title: El Niño Emerges in Pacific, Raising Heat and Crop Risks
item.description: El Niño has formed across the equatorial Pacific, setting the stage for months of droughts, floods and temperature fluctuations that will threaten communities worldwide along with agriculture and energy.

item.title: Solar Surpasses Coal in Historic Shift For US Electricity Mix
item.description: Solar overtook coal in US power generation in May, the first time the renewable source bested the fossil fuel in a calendar month.

item.title: Britain’s Garden Habits Are Making Their Homes Harder to Insure
item.description: As the UK moves from a record-hot May into an unseasonably wet June, the risk of flooding is being exacerbated by a proliferation of paved gardens, according to the country’s flood insurer of last resort.

item.title: Brookfield’s Neoen to Spend $7 Billion Doubling Australian Renewables Folio
item.description: French renewables developer Neoen SA expects to spend about A$10 billion ($7 billion) to more than double its Australian portfolio to 10 gigawatts by 2030.

item.title: How Singapore Uses District Cooling to Beat the Heat
item.description: Its part of a S$100 billion climate adaptation plan.

item.title: NYC Pensions Boss Says SpaceX’s Disregard for Shareholders Has ‘No Precedent’
item.description: New York City Comptroller Mark Levine says the unprecedented control that Elon Musk will have over SpaceX represents a new level of disregard for regular shareholders’ rights.

item.title: Revego and H1 Mull Merger for $807 Million Africa Renewables Fund
item.description: Revego Fund Managers is exploring a merger with H1 Holdings that would create one of South Africa’s largest renewable energy-focused funds.

item.title: Extreme Heat Risks Losses for Indian Suppliers to Uniqlo, Tesco
item.description: Garment factories in India supplying customers like Fast Retailing Co.’s Uniqlo, Marks & Spencer Plc and Tesco Plc are suffering productivity losses of as much as 10% as a result of the nation’s extreme heat, according to a new report.

item.title: Singapore Brings Back 19th-Century Tech to Beat Warming Climate
item.description: Deep underneath Singapore’s northeastern district of Punggol, a five-kilometer network of metal pipes roars as it pumps chilled water to cool offices and classrooms overhead.

item.title: Climate Change, Pollution Push Oceans to Tipping Point, UN Report Says
item.description: Dying coral reefs and depleted fish stocks are signs of the “grave risk” to marine health, according to the third World Ocean Assessment.

item.title: Wall Street Seeks Catastrophe Modelers and Weather Analysts
item.description: Some firms are seeking to expand their insurance-linked securities desks

item.title: PE Firm ARM-Harith Targets $200 Million for Africa Climate Fund
item.description: ARM-Harith Infrastructure Investments Ltd., a private equity fund manager, said it aims to raise $200 million to invest in energy and climate infrastructure projects across sub-Saharan Africa.

item.title: New York Just Got a Huge Influx of Clean Power. It Still Needs More
item.description: The Champlain Hudson Power Express carries abundant hydropower from Quebec to Queens. It only goes partway toward meeting the state’s fast-rising electricity demand.

item.title: El Niño Is Already Impacting India’s Critical Monsoon
item.description: Early signs of how El Niño may reshape global weather patterns are starting to show in the late onset and expected weakness of India’s monsoon rains.

item.title: Flood Warnings for South China Farmlands Raise Rice-Crop Risks
item.description: Forecasts for heavy rains in southern China over the next week are raising the risk of flash floods, potentially inundating crops and damaging rice fields.

item.title: Legal & General Says Energy Transition is ‘Alive and Well’
item.description: In a new report, the UK insurer and asset manager challenged the notion that the shift to a low-carbon economy has stalled.

item.title: Mexico Awards 37 Renewable Electricity Projects in Tender, Report Says
item.description: Mexico’s electricity commission concluded the first bidding process under its new mixed-investment scheme for renewable energy, to add an expected 7,411 megawatts of generation capacity, a local outlet reported.

item.title: Hedge Funds Are Expanding Desks Designed to Profit From Natural-Catastrophe Risk
item.description: When Mitesh Parikh, a headhunter at UK recruiter Selby Jennings, started looking for an ILS modeler for a large US hedge fund this year, the pool of qualified candidates was disconcertingly small.

item.title: China Issues Alert for Flood Risks in Hunan, Guangxi, Guizhou
item.description: China issued a red alert for mountain flooding on Sunday, warning that parts of southwestern Hunan, northern Guangxi and southeastern Guizhou face a high risk of flash floods triggered by heavy rainfall, according to a report by China Central Television.

item.title: US Commuters Turn to Public Transport as Gas Prices Stay High
item.description: High gas prices are contributing to a rise in public transit use.

item.title: The Albanian Island at the Center of Anger Over Kushner’s Resort Plans
item.description: The ride from the Albanian mainland to the island of Sazan takes just 20 minutes in a speedboat.

item.title: EU Plans Lower Taxes on Clean Energy in Drive to Cut Power Bills
item.description: The European Union is developing plans to cut taxes on renewable energy and make electricity systems more flexible as high costs continue to weigh on the region’s economy.

item.title: Trump’s Alaska Oil and Gas Lease Auction Draws Just Two Bidders
item.description: Only two bidders participated in the Trump administration’s auction for drilling leases in Alaska’s Arctic National Wildlife Refuge as big oil companies sat out the sale.

item.title: How Extreme Heat Will Factor Into World Cup Games
item.description: Some teams will face a much hotter path to the finals.
